meta.name: Prototype Pollution
meta.description: JavaScript prototype pollution payloads for client-side and server-side (Node.js) exploitation. Covers __proto__, constructor.prototype, JSON-based pollution, and gadget chains.

payloads.description: URL query pollution — sets Object.prototype.admin=1 via __proto__ query param
Dot-notation URL pollution — alternative to bracket notation
Constructor prototype pollution via URL — bypasses __proto__ string filters
Constructor dot-notation URL pollution
JSON body prototype pollution — merge/assign functions may propagate __proto__
Multi-property JSON pollution — sets multiple prototype properties at once
JSON constructor prototype pollution — avoids __proto__ literal check
Prototype pollution RCE gadget via template engine outputFunctionName (pug/jade)
Prototype pollution RCE via escapeFunction gadget — another template engine gadget
Prototype pollution via sourceURL gadget — node.js vm module source evaluation
Direct prototype pollution in JS console — for client-side testing
Prototype pollution verification — confirms pollution spreads to new empty objects
Fetch-based prototype pollution probe — sends polluting query param to API

payloads.code: const merge = (a, b) => { for (let k in b) a[k] = b[k]; return a; }; merge({}, JSON.parse('{"__proto__":{"polluted":true}}'));
payloads.description: Vulnerable merge function demo — for-in loop copies __proto__ key as own property
Lodash pre-4.17.12 prototype pollution via _.merge — classic CVE-2019-10744
deepmerge library prototype pollution — affects vulnerable versions
Node.js prototype pollution leading to RCE via NODE_OPTIONS pollution
Prototype pollution → Node.js debugger exposure via NODE_OPTIONS
Prototype pollution for HTTP request smuggling — pollutes default content-type headers
Form URL-encoded prototype pollution — works in POST body with qs or body-parser
